article_title: FDA Approves Regeneron's Otarmeni as First Gene Therapy for Genetic Hearing Loss, with the Drugmaker Pledging Free Access in the U.S.

findings.message: Source redirected to different domain: edition.cnn.com

content_preview.title: FDA Approves Regeneron's Otarmeni as First Gene Therapy for Genetic Hearing Loss, with the Drugmaker Pledging Free Access in the U.S.
content_preview.summary: Regeneron's one-time intracochlear gene therapy for OTOF-related deafness cleared the FDA 61 days after BLA filing, the first gene therapy product approved under the Commissioner's National Priority Voucher pilot program.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration on April 23, 2026, granted accelerated approval to Regeneron Pharmaceuticals' Otarmeni (lunsotogene parvec-cwha), making it the first gene therapy ever cleared in the United States for an inherited form of deafness. The decision, [announced by the FDA](https://www.fda.gov/news-events/press-announcements/fda-approves-first-ever-gene-therapy-treatment-genetic-hearing-loss-under-national-priority-voucher), arrived 61 days after Regeneron filed its ...

editor_notes.content_quality: Round-2 rewrite resolves all seven round-1 findings cleanly. Word count up to 954 (still within News range), structure intact, tone unchanged.
editor_notes.source_verification: Round-1 verification covered all 4 snapshots (FDA, Regeneron/GlobeNewswire, CNN, NBC) thoroughly. The seven re-attributions all map to passages already verified in round 1: 'fidelity' phrase to NBC (Shearer), 'five of 12 essentially restored' to CNN, '16/20 five months' to CNN, Yancopoulos title verbatim to Regeneron release, 'first gene therapy product' to FDA, voucher year dropped (was unsourced), cost categories replaced with Regeneron's verbatim 'out-of-pocket costs for administration' quote. Rewrite consolidates the Shearer 'fidelity' and 'on 24/7' quotes into a single NBC citation with Shearer named as the speaker and his Boston Children's Hospital affiliation included — strengthens attribution.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All seven round-1 attribution errors resolved. No new findings introduced.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Unchanged — neutral, professional, well-hedged on durability, free-access scope, patient identification. Adding Shearer's name and affiliation actually improves attribution rigor.
editor_notes.originality: Same as round 1 — new topic, valid Envoy Medical cross-link.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean round-2 fix on all seven findings. The rewrite also tightened the lede's free-access language to match Regeneron's actual hedge ('out-of-pocket costs for administration of the therapy fall outside its control') rather than the original paraphrase. APPROVE — ready to merge.
round_1_findings_resolved: 'Reduces the fidelity of the sound' — re-attributed to NBC (Shearer quote, with Shearer's name and Boston Children's Hospital affiliation now included).
'Five had their hearing essentially restored to normal' — re-attributed to CNN.
'16 of 20 / five months' — attributed to CNN (the source that pairs both figures).
Yancopoulos title — corrected verbatim to 'Board co-Chair, President and Chief Scientific Officer'; 'co-founder' dropped.
'First cell or gene therapy' — 'cell or' dropped in summary and body; now 'first gene therapy product'.
'Voucher program launched in 2025' — year dropped (was unsourced).
Specific uncovered cost categories — replaced with Regeneron's verbatim 'out-of-pocket costs for administration of this free therapy' quote.
